mnemonics: RMSprop and Adam: Runners Make Slow Progress And Don't Adapt
RMSprop and Adam: Rockets Making Slow Progress And Diving And Ascending
keyPoints: Key point 1: RMSprop adapts the learning rate based on the magnitude of the gradient, whereas Adam adapts both the learning rate and the momentum.
Key point 2: Adam uses two first moment estimates (m1 and m2) to compute the adaptive learning rate, while RMSprop uses only the first moment estimate.
Key point 3: RMSprop is simpler to implement and requires less memory than Adam, making it a popular choice for many applications.
